content.problemStatement:
Ash is trying to get visa to Chefland. For the visa to be approved, he needs to satisfy the following three criteria:


Solve at least x1x_1x1​ problems on Codechef.
Have at least y1y_1y1​ current rating on Codechef.
Make his last submission at most z1z_1z1​ months ago.
You are given the number of problems solved by Chef (x2x_2x2​), his current rating (y2y_2y2​) and the information that he made his last submission z2z_2z2​ months ago. Determine whether he will get the visa.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains six space-separated integers x1x_1x1​, x2x_2x2​, y1y_1y1​, y2y_2y2​, z1z_1z1​ and z2z_2z2​.
content.outputFormat:
For each test case, print a single line containing the string `"YES"` if Chef will get the visa or `"NO"` if he will not.

You may print each character of the string in uppercase or lowercase (for example, the strings "yEs", "yes", "Yes" and "YES" will all be treated as identical).

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: Chef's rating is less than the minimum required rating.
Example case 2: Chef has solved a smaller number of problems than the minimum required number.
Example case 3: Chef's last submission was made before the allowed period.
Example case 4: All three conditions are met.
